era_context: The mid-15th century was characterized by the final stages of the Hundred Years' War, the Council of Florence's ecumenical negotiations, the Ming Dynasty's consolidation, and the Ottoman Empire's expansion in the Eastern Mediterranean. This was a period of significant religious, political, and military transition across multiple civilizations.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Central Asia and Steppe regions (limited contemporary sources)

events.title: Council of Florence: Continued Ecumenical Negotiations
events.description: The Council of Florence continued its work in 1436 toward reconciliation between the Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches. Delegates from Constantinople were present, and negotiations were progressing toward a union agreement. The council was addressing fundamental theological differences. The negotiations would culminate in the union decree of 1439.
events.certainty_note: The council's proceedings are well-documented in official records.

events.title: Hundred Years' War: English Military Campaigns
events.description: The Hundred Years' War continued with English forces facing increasing pressure from French forces. By 1436, English control in France was contracting. The English were losing the military advantage they had held earlier in the century. This period saw the consolidation of French national identity and military strength under Charles VII.
key_figures: Henry VI (King of England, child during this period)

sources.name: Jean de Wavrin, Recueil des croniques et anchiennes istories de la Grant Bretaigne

events.certainty_note: The general trend of English losses during this period is well-attested, though specific events for 1436 are not always precisely dated.

events.title: Ming Dynasty: Xuande Emperor's Final Years
events.description: The Xuande Emperor of the Ming Dynasty was in the final years of his reign in 1436. He would die in early 1435 or 1436 (sources vary), and his young son would succeed him. The Xuande Emperor's reign (1426–1435/1436) was characterized by administrative consolidation and the continuation of the Zheng He voyages, which were winding down. The emperor's death would mark a transition to a regency period under eunuch influence.
events.certainty_note: The Xuande Emperor's reign and death are well-documented in Ming official records, though the exact date of his death varies slightly between sources.
cross_references: Leads to regency period under Zhengtong Emperor

events.title: Portuguese Exploration: Cape Bojador Expeditions
events.description: Portuguese explorers, sponsored by Prince Henry the Navigator, continued systematic exploration of the West African coast in 1436. These expeditions were gathering information about African geography, trade routes, and indigenous peoples. The voyages were motivated by desires for gold, spices, and knowledge of African kingdoms. Portuguese caravels were becoming more sophisticated, allowing for longer voyages.

sources.name: Gomes de Zurara, Chronicle of the Discovery and Conquest of Guinea

events.certainty_note: Portuguese exploration of the African coast was ongoing during this period, but specific events for 1436 are not always precisely dated in sources.
cross_references: Part of Portuguese Age of Exploration (1415–1522)

events.title: Ottoman Empire: Consolidation in the Balkans
events.description: The Ottoman Empire under Murad II continued its consolidation of power in the Balkans. By 1436, Ottoman control extended over much of the former Serbian and Bulgarian territories. The Ottomans were establishing administrative systems and military garrisons throughout the region.

sources.name: Ottoman court records and administrative documents

events.certainty_note: Ottoman expansion in the Balkans during this period is well-attested, though specific events for 1436 are not always precisely dated.

disconfirming_evidence: The exact date of the Xuande Emperor's death is disputed between sources, with some placing it in 1435 and others in early 1436. This creates uncertainty about the precise year of the Zhengtong Emperor's succession. The Council of Florence's progress in 1436 is not always clearly distinguished from 1437 and 1438 in sources, as the negotiations were continuous.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 1436 is moderate but unevenly distributed. European sources are relatively abundant, particularly for religious and political events. Chinese sources are rich but require specialized knowledge. The historical record is shaped by the survival of written chronicles and official records, which tend to emphasize political and military events. There is a significant Western and European bias in available English-language sources.

graph_edges.note: The negotiations of 1436 led to the union decree of 1439
The Xuande Emperor's death in 1435/1436 led to the succession of the Zhengtong Emperor
